narration_script: Two weeks into Operation Epic Fury, the military campaign to dismantle Iran’s ballistic and nuclear capabilities has triggered a global crisis. While the initial air assault targeted air defenses and naval assets, the Iranian regime remains in power and is lashing out. The conflict has rapidly escalated across the Middle East, with Iranian missiles striking Israel and American bases. Beyond the immediate combat zones, the humanitarian toll is staggering. The United Nations reports that over three million people are now displaced as civilians flee major cities. Economically, the world is reeling as the Strait of Hormuz remains effectively choked off, disrupting twenty percent of the global oil supply. In response to surging costs, European governments are moving to crack down on price gouging by energy firms. This escalation has left Washington’s Gulf allies in a state of profound shock, as regional stability evaporates. Despite U.S. efforts to pivot away from the Middle East, this conflict proves the region continues to dominate American foreign policy.

key_points.label: Operation Epic Fury Begins
key_points.excerpt: A campaign to rid Iran of its ballistic missiles, navy and any lingering ambitions to develop a nuclear weapon has triggered a crisis which threatens regional stability and the global economy.
key_points.script_anchor: campaign to dismantle Iran’s ballistic and nuclear capabilities

key_points.label: Conflict Escalates Regionally
key_points.excerpt: The conflict quickly escalated, with Iran attacking Israel and American bases across the region with missiles and drones.
key_points.script_anchor: Iranian missiles striking Israel and American bases

key_points.label: Massive Civilian Displacement
key_points.excerpt: The UN says as many as 3.2 million people have been displaced so far, as Iranians - and many long-term Afghan refugees - flee cities and seek shelter in the countryside.
key_points.script_anchor: over three million people are now displaced

key_points.label: Global Oil Supply Choked
key_points.excerpt: The Strait of Hormuz, a narrow waterway through which about 20% of the world’s oil passes, has been all-but choked off.
key_points.script_anchor: Strait of Hormuz remains effectively choked off

key_points.label: Crackdown on Price Gouging
key_points.excerpt: In Britain and Europe, governments say they will crack down on price gouging, amid fears that energy companies are seeking to profit from the crisis.
key_points.script_anchor: crack down on price gouging by energy firms

key_points.label: Gulf Allies Express Shock
key_points.excerpt: For Washington’s Gulf allies, including Saudi Arabia and the UAE, the war has come as a profound shock.
key_points.script_anchor: Washington’s Gulf allies in a state of profound shock

key_points.label: Middle East Dominates Policy
key_points.excerpt: The war in Iran demonstrates, yet again, that the wider Middle East has a way of sucking America back in.
key_points.script_anchor: region continues to dominate American foreign policy
